title: WebSocket vs SSE vs long-polling
description: These differ in directionality, proxy friendliness, reconnect support, and operational cost; pick by actual need.
body: Use when selecting a realtime transport. Long-polling is the most compatible but inefficient and high-latency. SSE is server-to-client only, runs over HTTP, auto-reconnects with Last-Event-ID, and is proxy-friendly, but most browsers cap connections per origin. WebSocket is bidirectional and low-latency but needs upgrade support through proxies/load balancers and its own reconnect and auth-refresh logic. Do not pick WebSocket reflexively for one-way streams. Actions: match transport to directionality, latency, and infra. Validation signals include confirming the chosen transport survives your proxies and reconnects correctly. Risk boundary: a transport that your load balancer silently buffers or times out breaks realtime guarantees.
